673c03f04d9b6b7ed1ef5875.question: Two answers required. A letter from one of these people to the other blames “Christian Priests, Jewish Rabbis, Grecian Sages and Roman Emperors” for the loss of history of nations more ancient than Christ. The views of race and inequality of these two people are discussed in sections labeled “then” and “now” in the first two parts of Joseph Ellis’ book American Dialogue. Along with one of their wives, letters between these two people were comprehensively compiled by Lester J. Cappon. In a letter to one of these people, the other claimed that “the same political (*) parties which now agitate the U.S. have existed through all time”. These people spent 15 years exchanging heavily studied letters after Benjamin Rush mended their friendship. The last words of one of these people was that the other survives, a claim made ironic as they both died within hours of each other on July 4, 1826. For 10 points, name these two people, the second and third president of the United States.
673c03f04d9b6b7ed1ef5875.question_sanitized: Two answers required. A letter from one of these people to the other blames "Christian Priests, Jewish Rabbis, Grecian Sages and Roman Emperors" for the loss of history of nations more ancient than Christ. The views of race and inequality of these two people are discussed in sections labeled "then" and "now" in the first two parts of Joseph Ellis' book American Dialogue. Along with one of their wives, letters between these two people were comprehensively compiled by Lester J. Cappon. In a letter to one of these people, the other claimed that "the same political (*) parties which now agitate the U.S. have existed through all time". These people spent 15 years exchanging heavily studied letters after Benjamin Rush mended their friendship. The last words of one of these people was that the other survives, a claim made ironic as they both died within hours of each other on July 4, 1826. For 10 points, name these two people, the second and third president of the United States.
673c03f04d9b6b7ed1ef5875.answer: John Adams and Thomas Jefferson [don’t accept John Quincy Adams, of course]
673c03f04d9b6b7ed1ef5875.answer_sanitized: John Adams and Thomas Jefferson [don't accept John Quincy Adams, of course]

673c03f04d9b6b7ed1ef5882.question: Traveler Johann Georg Keyssler wrote that rulers seeking the support of these people had to maintain the “three Fs” of food, festivities, and public executions. Visitors to one city during the 19th century like Goethe would fascinatingly watch these people eating massive amounts of boiled food with their hands in a single bite. Engels blamed Swiss mercenaries and these “greedy” people for the quick capture of one city by reactionaries during the first revolution sparked in 1848, which had secured the (*) Albertine Statute. These people were the largest group behind a revolution led by the fishermen Masaniello that created a French-supported republic in 1647. Lingering monarchist belief and street fighting among these people is often blamed for the failure of the Parthenopean Republic and the subsequent restoration of its Bourbon king Ferdinand. For 10 points, name this surprisingly powerful lowest social class of early modern Naples.
673c03f04d9b6b7ed1ef5882.question_sanitized: Traveler Johann Georg Keyssler wrote that rulers seeking the support of these people had to maintain the "three Fs" of food, festivities, and public executions. Visitors to one city during the 19th century like Goethe would fascinatingly watch these people eating massive amounts of boiled food with their hands in a single bite. Engels blamed Swiss mercenaries and these "greedy" people for the quick capture of one city by reactionaries during the first revolution sparked in 1848, which had secured the (*) Albertine Statute. These people were the largest group behind a revolution led by the fishermen Masaniello that created a French-supported republic in 1647. Lingering monarchist belief and street fighting among these people is often blamed for the failure of the Parthenopean Republic and the subsequent restoration of its Bourbon king Ferdinand. For 10 points, name this surprisingly powerful lowest social class of early modern Naples.
673c03f04d9b6b7ed1ef5882.answer: lazzaroni [accept lazzari; prompt on descriptive answers of the poor of Naples or equivalents]

673c03f04d9b6b7ed1ef5896.question: Almost all women were restricted from entering this place by local officials called otona, who led a group of locals supervising visitors. One country’s first female doctor was born to botanist Philipp Franz von Siebold after he met a woman in this place, though he was expelled after acquiring illegal maps. During the Napoleonic Wars, this place was briefly occupied by the British ship Phaeton until they were resupplied; the end of that incident was negotiated by this place’s local opperhoofd Hendrik Doeff. This place was the first point of exposure for most (*) rangaku knowledge After the Shimabara Rebellion, the Portuguese were replaced in this place with closely monitored men from the VOC, leading to two centuries in which it was the only place where Europeans could live. For 10 points, name this place where Dutch traders were restricted during the Tokugawa period, an artificial island in the harbour of Nagasaki.
673c03f04d9b6b7ed1ef5896.question_sanitized: Almost all women were restricted from entering this place by local officials called otona, who led a group of locals supervising visitors. One country's first female doctor was born to botanist Philipp Franz von Siebold after he met a woman in this place, though he was expelled after acquiring illegal maps. During the Napoleonic Wars, this place was briefly occupied by the British ship Phaeton until they were resupplied; the end of that incident was negotiated by this place's local opperhoofd Hendrik Doeff. This place was the first point of exposure for most (*) rangaku knowledge After the Shimabara Rebellion, the Portuguese were replaced in this place with closely monitored men from the VOC, leading to two centuries in which it was the only place where Europeans could live. For 10 points, name this place where Dutch traders were restricted during the Tokugawa period, an artificial island in the harbour of Nagasaki.

673c03f04d9b6b7ed1ef58b3.question: Description acceptable. During the second of these trials, the defendant’s lawyer compared thinking about banking laws in a church to his thoughts looking at a George Washington picture behind the judge. In the prelude to the first of these trials, a woman claimed “girls lean back everywhere” when writing to John S. Sumner. In an appeal to the second of these trials, two judges of the Hand family agreed to deliberately write a boring opinion to avoid media coverage. The first of these trials ruled against lesbian editors Anderson and Heap of (*) The Little Review based on the Hicklin test; that application of the Comstock Act was prompted by publication of the “Nausicaa” episode. John M. Woolsey’s opinion about “old Saxon words” in one of these trials was widely published in subsequent editions of the book he had declared not pornographic. For 10 points, name these trials over a sexually charged book published in 1922 by an Irish novelist.

John M. Woolsey's opinion about "old Saxon words" in one of these trials was widely published in subsequent editions of the book he had declared not pornographic. 673c03f04d9b6b7ed1ef58b3.answer: the Ulysses obscenity trials [accept answers involving censorship or restriction on the publication of Ulysses; accept United States v. One Book Called Ulysses; prompt on answers involving Ulysses trials or the James Joyce publication trials and ask for the offense they dealt with]

673c03f04d9b6b7ed1ef58b6.question: The sister of a leader of this country, a politician with the first name Jessie, implied he was racist in the pamphlet “Beware My Brother”. A leader of this country detailed British and American efforts to destabilize postcolonial democracies in the memoir The West on Trial. While training as a dentist at Northwestern, that leader of this country met his American wife Janet who would succeed him in 1997. The banning of a historian from this country from teaching after returning from a conference in (*) Montreal led to 1968 riots at the UWI. A historian from this country wrote the book How Europe Underdeveloped Africa while teaching in Dar es Salaam in the early 1970s. The historian Walter Rodney was from this country and was assassinated in 1980 on the orders of its president, the rival of Cheddi Jagan. For 10 points, name this South American country led by Forbes Burnham where party politics has been split between African and Indian descended people.
673c03f04d9b6b7ed1ef58b6.question_sanitized: The sister of a leader of this country, a politician with the first name Jessie, implied he was racist in the pamphlet "Beware My Brother". A leader of this country detailed British and American efforts to destabilize postcolonial democracies in the memoir The West on Trial. While training as a dentist at Northwestern, that leader of this country met his American wife Janet who would succeed him in 1997. The banning of a historian from this country from teaching after returning from a conference in (*) Montreal led to 1968 riots at the UWI. A historian from this country wrote the book How Europe Underdeveloped Africa while teaching in Dar es Salaam in the early 1970s. The historian Walter Rodney was from this country and was assassinated in 1980 on the orders of its president, the rival of Cheddi Jagan. For 10 points, name this South American country led by Forbes Burnham where party politics has been split between African and Indian descended people.
673c03f04d9b6b7ed1ef58b6.answer_sanitized: Guyana [or the Co-operative Republic of Guyana]

673c03f04d9b6b7ed1ef58c6.question: 10 quite large examples of these places were found by Wilfrid Laurier University archaeologists at the Moyer site. During the 18th century, traditional designs for these places declined in communities like German Flatts settled by Palatine migrants paying rent. An important source on social rules and construction of these places is the writing of the Jesuit missionary Joseph-François Lafitau. One group’s autonym translating as “people of [these places]” was preempted by a name derived from their term (*) “hiro” or another group’s word for “rattlesnake”. Wendat designs for these places informed a reconstruction at St. Marie Among the Hurons with cedar bark for sheathing. Ceremonial roles as keepers of western and eastern windows to these places were held by the Seneca and Mohawk, as the Six Nations of the Haudenosaunee [hah-doo-wah-saw-ne] considered their territory as one large example of them. For 10 points, name these wooden structures built for the matrilineal groups central to historical Iroquois villages.
673c03f04d9b6b7ed1ef58c6.question_sanitized: 10 quite large examples of these places were found by Wilfrid Laurier University archaeologists at the Moyer site. During the 18th century, traditional designs for these places declined in communities like German Flatts settled by Palatine migrants paying rent. An important source on social rules and construction of these places is the writing of the Jesuit missionary Joseph-Francois Lafitau. One group's autonym translating as "people of [these places]" was preempted by a name derived from their term (*) "hiro" or another group's word for "rattlesnake". Wendat designs for these places informed a reconstruction at St. Marie Among the Hurons with cedar bark for sheathing. Ceremonial roles as keepers of western and eastern windows to these places were held by the Seneca and Mohawk, as the Six Nations of the Haudenosaunee [hah-doo-wah-saw-ne] considered their territory as one large example of them. For 10 points, name these wooden structures built for the matrilineal groups central to historical Iroquois villages.
673c03f04d9b6b7ed1ef58c6.answer: Iroquois longhouses [or Haudenosaunee longhouses; accept Ganoñhsésgeh; prompt on houses; prompt on villages until read]
673c03f04d9b6b7ed1ef58c6.answer_sanitized: Iroquois longhouses [or Haudenosaunee longhouses; accept Ganonhsesgeh; prompt on houses; prompt on villages until read]

673c03f14d9b6b7ed1ef58db.question: Description acceptable. Shortly before this event, a small group of American saboteurs used contaminants to destroy bus engines and dropped leaflets describing a three day worker’s holiday. During a 300-day grace period after this event, hundreds of thousands of people including many Catholics moved to one country during the Operation Passage to Freedom, while “Revolutionary Regroupees” moved in the opposite direction. Planned elections that would reverse this event were stalled by the leader of the (*) Personality Labor Revolutionary Party, as he would likely have lost them convincingly. To balance American and Chinese interests, this event was meant to be reversed two years after the 1954 Geneva Conference, which created an ostensibly temporary border along the 17th parallel that was only abolished in 1976. For 10 points, name or describe this event that occurred alongside the end of French Indochina that was reversed after the fall of Saigon.

Planned elections that would reverse this event were stalled by the leader of the (*) Personality Labor Revolutionary Party, as he would likely have lost them convincingly. To balance American and Chinese interests, this event was meant to be reversed two years after the 1954 Geneva Conference, which created an ostensibly temporary border along the 17th parallel that was only abolished in 1976. For 10 points, name or describe this event that occurred alongside the end of French Indochina that was reversed after the fall of Saigon.
673c03f14d9b6b7ed1ef58db.answer: the partition of Vietnam [accept synonyms for partition; answers involving the creation of North and South Vietnam as separate countries; prompt on answers involving the French leaving Vietnam and ask “what related change was made alongside it?”]

673c03f14d9b6b7ed1ef5911.question: During a conference near Manchester, this leader unsuccessfully proposed to Marcus Garvey’s widow Amy. During one civil war, a city named for this leader was the main base of the LURD rebel group. This leader’s capital names an informal anti-socialist group that sought less integration than the Casablanca Group during the lead-up to the foundation of the OAU. Concessions to the Bong Mining Company and Firestone were made during this leader’s (*) “open door” economic policy that prospered during the 1960s. This leader’s surname came from an Atlanta philanthropist Emily, a friend of Henry Clay who sponsored 69 migrants with the ACS. This leader, succeeded by his Vice President Tolbert with the same given name, entered his country into World War II as leader of the True Whig Party, the longest era of its one party rule. For 10 points, name this modernizing leader who served 27 years as President of Liberia.

Concessions to the Bong Mining Company and Firestone were made during this leader's (*) "open door" economic policy that prospered during the 1960s. This leader's surname came from an Atlanta philanthropist Emily, a friend of Henry Clay who sponsored 69 migrants with the ACS. This leader, succeeded by his Vice President Tolbert with the same given name, entered his country into World War II as leader of the True Whig Party, the longest era of its one party rule. For 10 points, name this modernizing leader who served 27 years as President of Liberia.

673c03f14d9b6b7ed1ef5915.question: While in a car with the American president, a holder of this position joked that he should have asked his driver to run over protesting communists blocking the route. The world’s first greenhouse gas trading system was created under a holder of this position. During a 1932 constitutional crisis, a holder of this position was dismissed by governor Phillip Game over a plan that would cancel interest payments to international bondholders. During the Great Depression, the federal government’s financial plans were opposed by (*) Jack Lang, a holder of this position who led a split faction of the Labor Party. The first Liberal Party holder of this position was Robert Askin, who joined Prime Minister Harold Holt in welcoming LBJ during a state visit and clashed with architect Jørn Utzon over the expensive construction of a now iconic venue. For 10 points, name this state leadership position whose holder Bob Carr oversaw the planning of the 2000 Olympics.

The world's first greenhouse gas trading system was created under a holder of this position. During a 1932 constitutional crisis, a holder of this position was dismissed by governor Phillip Game over a plan that would cancel interest payments to international bondholders. During the Great Depression, the federal government's financial plans were opposed by (*) Jack Lang, a holder of this position who led a split faction of the Labor Party. The first Liberal Party holder of this position was Robert Askin, who joined Prime Minister Harold Holt in welcoming LBJ during a state visit and clashed with architect Jorn Utzon over the expensive construction of a now iconic venue. For 10 points, name this state leadership position whose holder Bob Carr oversaw the planning of the 2000 Olympics.
673c03f14d9b6b7ed1ef5915.answer_sanitized: Premier of New South Wales [or Premier of NSW]
